summary: Iteration 72: Verified SERVICE-LEVEL credit gate implementation. All 24 backend tests passed. Code review confirms: (1) AIService._creditApproved flag initialized to false, (2) approveCreditGate() sets flag to true, (3) _requireCreditApproval() throws ProviderError(billing) if flag is false, (4) callModelSafely() and streamModelSafely() wrappers enforce credit gate and translate errors, (5) All provider methods (processMessageStream, processMessage, executePlanStream, processImageGeneration) call _requireCreditApproval(), (6) Route handlers (stream-handler.js, chats.js, diffs.js) call approveCreditGate() after credit balance check, (7) image-service.js uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY with proxy options, (8) Provider status endpoint only checks key existence.
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ /app/lib/ai/service.js: callModelSafely() enforces credit gate + translates errors via classifyProviderError (lines 107-115)
✓ /app/lib/ai/service.js: streamModelSafely() enforces credit gate + translates errors (lines 121-129)
✓ /app/lib/api/stream-handler.js: aiService.approveCreditGate() called after credit balance check (line 134)
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/chats.js: Fork handler correctly creates AIService without approveCreditGate (only uses compressContext, no provider interaction)
✓ /app/lib/ai/image-service.js: Uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY as primary key with EMERGENT_PROXY_URL for proxy (lines 164-176)
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/public.js: Provider status only checks key existence, never pings APIs (lines 18-41)
✓ /app/lib/ai/errors.js: classifyProviderError translates all raw errors to safe user-facing messages
✓ No raw_error or API key details exposed in any error response
context_for_next_testing_agent: Service-level credit gate is fully implemented. Key defense-in-depth pattern: (1) Route handlers check credit balance first, (2) If balance sufficient, call aiService.approveCreditGate(), (3) AIService methods call _requireCreditApproval() before any provider interaction, (4) If _creditApproved is false, throws ProviderError(billing). This ensures even if a route handler forgets credit check, the AIService itself blocks the provider call. Fork handler is an exception - it creates AIService without approveCreditGate because it only calls compressContext (no provider interaction).

frontend_tests.login_form: PASS - Email and password inputs visible
frontend_tests.no_errors: PASS - No error messages on page

aiservice_credit_gate.callModelSafely_wrapper: ✓ Enforces credit gate + translates errors via classifyProviderError

route_handlers_call_approve_credit_gate.fork_handler: ✓ Correctly does NOT call approveCreditGate (only uses compressContext)

error_sanitization.no_api_key_exposure: ✓ No API key patterns in responses
